expectations.text: Explains that $(document).ready fails because the OA framework loads jQuery after the script files execute, so jQuery isn't available at script parse time
expectations.evidence: 'The OA framework loads jQuery after your control's static .js file has already been parsed and executed.' Baseline read the existing JS patterns.md reference.

expectations.text: Recommends Sys.Application.add_load as the correct OA replacement for $(document).ready
expectations.evidence: Full section 'The Correct Pattern: Sys.Application.add_load' with var $ = jQuery alias. Baseline found POGSplit.js as the canonical example.

expectations.text: Mentions that a jQuery IIFE wrapper (e.g. (function($){...})(jQuery)) also fails for the same reason — not just the document.ready form
expectations.evidence: '(function($){...}(jQuery)) immediately-invoked form also fails — same reason.' Explicitly covered.

expectations.text: If mentioning code-behind script injection, recommends ScriptManager.RegisterClientScriptBlock rather than Page.ClientScript (which is silently ignored when ScriptManager is present)
expectations.evidence: 'Do not use Page.ClientScript.RegisterClientScriptBlock — it is silently ignored when a ScriptManager is on the page'. ScriptManager.RegisterClientScriptBlock shown. Baseline read patterns.md reference.
